 Project Hyperspeed, Daily
<< 2025 07 21 copy
August 25, 2025

Time Flies

Table of Contents

  1. What I’ve Been Up to Lately
    1. Projects

When you don’t document your life, time slips away. It feels like just yesterday I recorded the last podcast on Web3TV. It almost seems like my podcast faded along with my youth. I guess that’s why I’m here. It’s time for me to start doing something consistently again. That last sentence felt a bit cringe as I typed it out. I see others pursuing their passions, and I can’t help but think I could do it better. I miss the daily grind.

What I’ve Been Up to Lately

This year, I’ve traveled more than ever before, moving almost as far across the lower 48 as physically possible—part of some divine plan, I suppose. I flew to Florida in December, then to Indianapolis shortly after, and eventually settled in Florida. I also made a trip back to Seattle, with at least two more planned before the year ends. Life feels different now. It doesn’t matter where I am as long as I have internet access, a seed phrase, and a weekly check. I’m still bummed about some past decisions, but wealth has never felt closer.

My life changed when I bought my Milady. That led me to Remilio, where I minted around 18. At one point, my Remilio portfolio made up 99% of my assets. I dove into Fumo, but things have been illiquid and tough—not as bad as I feared, though. After tallying it up, I still hold about $20,000 in Remilio assets. I’m confident this will be huge within a few years. Right now, I’m focused on stacking more ETH and buying as much CULT as I can, aiming for that golden parachute at a $900 million valuation. As the second-largest liquidity coin on Ethereum, I believe it’s one of the most exciting plays in crypto history.

Projects

All my projects can be found on my GitHub: Cmoneycodes

I created an ERC-404 bot that tracks mint events and posts them to Twitter.
